name: Web Tools
description: Domain health monitoring — DNS, SSL, HTTP headers grade, blacklist 30 DNSBL, WHOIS, SEO, email SPF/DMARC/DKIM, geo probes from 4 regions, port scans, SMTP test, recurring automated monitors with alerts.

tools.name: skeleton_refresh_web_tools
tools.description: Web Tools monitor health summary — critical/warning/ok counts only. For DNS lookups, domain audits, geo checks and all diagnostic tools use dns_lookup / domain_full_check / geo_check — they do NOT require monitors.

tools.name: dns_lookup
tools.description: DNS record lookup — use this for ANY request about DNS records of a domain. IMPORTANT: use record_type='all' when user asks for 'all DNS records', 'show all', 'all types' — returns A+AAAA+MX+NS+TXT+CNAME+SRV in ONE call. Use record_type='propagation' + propagation_type='NS' when user says 'NS records updated, did they propagate?', 'did my DNS update?', 'already propagated?'. Use specific type (MX/TXT/NS/A) only when user asks for that specific type. Use authoritative=true to query domain's own NS directly. This function does NOT need any monitors to exist — works on any domain instantly.

authoritative.description: Query from domain's own authoritative NS (A/AAAA/MX/NS/TXT/CNAME)
authoritative.title: Authoritative

propagation_type.description: Record type for propagation check (default: A). Use NS when user updated NS records, MX for mail, TXT for SPF/DMARC.
propagation_type.title: Propagation Type

return_schema.description: Generic domain diagnostic — DNS, WHOIS, HTTP, SEO, Network.
return_schema.title: DomainCheckResult

tools.name: ssl_check
tools.description: SSL certificate QUALITY — grade A-F, days until expiry, issuer, TLS version support. Full mode adds chain, SANs, fingerprint. Use this to answer 'how good is my SSL cert?'. To check if SSL is accessible FROM different world regions, use geo_check with check_type=ssl instead.

full.description: Full check — adds chain, SANs, fingerprint, TLS version support
full.title: Full

return_schema.description: SSL/TLS certificate check result.
return_schema.title: SslResult

tools.name: whois_lookup
tools.description: WHOIS ownership data — for domains: registrar, creation/expiry dates, nameservers, status. For IPs: owner org, ASN, country, abuse contact. Use target_type=ip for IP addresses. NOTE: for IP geolocation (city, latitude, latitude) use network_check with check_type=ip_lookup instead.

return_schema.description: Generic domain diagnostic — DNS, WHOIS, HTTP, SEO, Network.
return_schema.title: DomainCheckResult

tools.name: http_check
tools.description: HTTP security headers QUALITY — grade A+ to F (HSTS/CSP/X-Frame-Options/X-Content-Type-Options), missing headers with fix tips, redirect chain, response time. Use to answer 'how secure are my HTTP headers?'. To check if site responds from different world regions, use geo_check with check_type=http instead.

check_type.description: headers=full analysis, grade=score only, quick=grade+counts, missing=broken headers+fixes, status, redirects
check_type.title: Check Type

return_schema.description: Generic domain diagnostic — DNS, WHOIS, HTTP, SEO, Network.
return_schema.title: DomainCheckResult

tools.name: network_check
tools.description: Network diagnostics. Works with BOTH domains and IPs: ping (latency/packet loss), traceroute (per-hop MTR path). Works with IP ADDRESSES ONLY — never use on a domain name: ip_lookup (geolocation: city/lat/lon/country — different from whois_lookup which gives ownership), reverse_dns (PTR record — who is this IP?), asn (ASN WHOIS prefix list). Example: ping for 'amplica.md', ip_lookup for '104.18.15.2'.

check_type.description: ping=RTT/loss, traceroute=MTR per-hop, reverse_dns=PTR, ip_lookup=geo+ASN, asn=ASN WHOIS
check_type.title: Check Type

return_schema.description: Generic domain diagnostic — DNS, WHOIS, HTTP, SEO, Network.
return_schema.title: DomainCheckResult

tools.name: seo_check
tools.description: SEO check — meta title/description length and issues, robots.txt rules, sitemap.xml validation, Google indexing status. Use check_type to target a specific area.

target.description: Domain or full URL (full URL for meta check)
target.title: Target

check_type.description: meta=title/description/issues, robots=robots.txt rules, sitemap=sitemap.xml, indexing=Google status
check_type.title: Check Type

return_schema.description: Generic domain diagnostic — DNS, WHOIS, HTTP, SEO, Network.
return_schema.title: DomainCheckResult

tools.name: run_scan
tools.description: Trigger an immediate scan for an EXISTING MONITOR (requires monitor_id from list_monitors). Scans ALL domains in that monitor's group. Use ONLY when user explicitly mentions a monitor by name or asks to 'run monitor', 'rescan monitor', 'refresh monitor now'. Do NOT use this for ad-hoc domain checks — for that use domain_full_check instead.

monitor_id.description: Monitor ID to run scan for now
monitor_id.title: Monitor Id

return_schema.description: Domain monitor scan snapshot result.
return_schema.title: MonitorScanResult

tools.name: get_scan_results
tools.description: Get last snapshot results for an EXISTING MONITOR (requires monitor_id). Shows per-domain/per-check status from the most recent scheduled scan. Use ONLY when user asks about a specific monitor's results or history. Do NOT use for ad-hoc domain checks — for that use domain_full_check.

monitor_id.description: Monitor ID to retrieve last scan results for
monitor_id.title: Monitor Id

return_schema.description: Domain monitor scan snapshot result.
return_schema.title: MonitorScanResult

tools.name: email_check
tools.description: Email authentication — SPF/DMARC/DKIM/BIMI checks or combined A-F grade, trace raw email headers to find originating IP, generate SPF or DMARC records.

check_type.description: spf/dmarc/dkim/bimi=individual check, full=grade A-F, trace=parse raw headers, generate_spf/generate_dmarc=build record
check_type.title: Check Type

return_schema.description: Email authentication check — SPF, DMARC, DKIM, BIMI, full grade.
return_schema.title: EmailAuthResult

tools.name: blacklist_check
tools.description: Spam blacklist check — IP against 30 DNSBL lists (Spamhaus, SpamCop, Barracuda) or domain against SURBL. Returns verdict: clean / listed / critical.

target_type.description: 'ip' checks 30 DNSBL in parallel, 'domain' resolves to IP then checks SURBL
target_type.title: Target Type

return_schema.description: Spam blacklist check — DNSBL/SURBL verdict.
return_schema.title: BlacklistResult

tools.name: port_scan
tools.description: TCP port check — single port status (open/closed/filtered) or preset scan: web (80/443/8080/8443), mail (25/587/465/993/995), database (3306/5432/6379), all.

port.description: Specific port to check (0 = preset scan)
port.title: Port

return_schema.description: TCP port scan result.
return_schema.title: PortScanResult

tools.name: smtp_test
tools.description: SMTP server test — connects via MX record or direct host, verifies EHLO/STARTTLS/AUTH support, reads server banner. Use to diagnose email delivery problems.

target.description: Domain name (uses MX) or specific mail server hostname
target.title: Target

port.description: Specific port to test (0 = auto-try 587/25/465 via MX)
port.title: Port

return_schema.description: SMTP server connectivity test result.
return_schema.title: SmtpResult

tools.name: geo_check
tools.description: Geographic reachability — probes domain FROM 4 world regions (EU/US/SG/MD). ROUTING RULES — pick check_type based on what the user is asking: → 'loading speed', 'response time', 'how fast from X country' → check_type=http (HTTP response time + status per region). → 'latency', 'ping', 'ms from region', 'reachable from X' → check_type=ping (RTT ms + packet loss per region). → 'dns from different countries', 'dns propagation per region', 'different DNS results per location' → check_type=dns. → 'ssl from different regions', 'ssl reachable from US' → check_type=ssl. → 'traceroute', 'network path', 'routing path from region' → check_type=traceroute. → 'full geo audit', 'all geo checks', 'complete geo report' → check_type=full (runs dns+http+ssl from all 4 regions simultaneously — slowest, only use when explicitly requested). NEVER default to check_type=full for a simple speed or reachability question — use http or ping. NOTE: this tool tests REACHABILITY and SPEED from regions only. For SSL certificate quality/grade → use ssl_check. For HTTP security headers grade → use http_check. For DNS record values → use dns_lookup.

check_type.description: Probe from EU/US/SG/MD: dns=resolution+mismatch, ping=latency, http=status, ssl=validity, traceroute, full=dns+http+ssl
check_type.title: Check Type

return_schema.description: Multi-region geo probe result (EU/US/SG/MD).
return_schema.title: GeoCheckResult

tools.name: domain_full_check
tools.description: INSTANT one-shot domain audit — no monitors or setup required, works on ANY domain. Runs selected checks in parallel and shows a summary table. Use this when: user mentions a domain and asks for 'full check', 'analysis', 'audit', 'show everything', 'what can you do on this domain', 'check this site'. Default checks: dns + ssl (certificate grade) + http (security headers grade) + email (SPF/DMARC/DKIM) + blacklist (spam lists). Optional extra checks: 'geo' (reachability from EU/US/SG/MD), 'whois' (ownership), 'seo' (meta/title issues), 'ports' (open ports), 'smtp' (mail-server connectivity). Returns a compact per-check status summary by default; set include_raw=true only when the user explicitly wants the full raw data. Do NOT use run_scan, list_monitors or get_scan_results for ad-hoc domain checks — those are for recurring monitor automation only.

checks.description: Checks to run in parallel. Defaults to dns+ssl+http+email+blacklist. Add 'geo' for multi-region reachability (slowest), 'whois' for ownership, 'seo' for meta/title issues, 'ports' for open-port scan, 'smtp' for mail-server connectivity.
checks.title: Checks

include_raw.description: When false (default) the data payload carries only per-check status + a one-line summary (compact). Set true to also return the full raw check results (large — only when explicitly asked for raw data).
include_raw.title: Include Raw

return_schema.description: Full parallel domain audit — all check results combined.
return_schema.title: DomainAuditResult

tools.name: create_domain_group
tools.description: Create a named group of domains for monitoring (max 5 groups, max 20 domains each). Required before creating a monitor with create_monitor.

domains.description: Domain names (max 20). From TagInput or chat.
domains.title: Domains

return_schema.description: Named group of domains for monitoring.
return_schema.title: DomainGroupEntity

tools.name: update_domain_group
tools.description: Add, remove or replace domains in an existing group, or rename it (max 20 domains). Use list_domain_groups first to get the group_id.

domains.description: Full replacement domain list (from TagInput panel edit)
domains.title: Domains

return_schema.description: Named group of domains for monitoring.
return_schema.title: DomainGroupEntity

tools.name: list_domain_groups
tools.description: Show all domain groups — names, domain lists and domain count. Call before create_monitor to pick the right group_id.

params_schema.description: No parameters — satisfies V17 for parameterless handlers.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

DomainGroupEntity.description: Named group of domains for monitoring.
DomainGroupEntity.title: DomainGroupEntity

return_schema.description: Paginated domain group list — returned by list_domain_groups().
return_schema.title: DomainGroupPage

tools.name: delete_domain_group
tools.description: Permanently delete a domain group and cascade-delete all monitors that use it. Cannot be undone — confirm group_id with list_domain_groups first.

return_schema.description: Generic CRUD confirmation — create/update/delete operations.
return_schema.title: WtOpResult

tools.name: create_check_profile
tools.description: Create a check profile — defines which checks (ssl/http/email/blacklist/geo/whois/dns) run per domain in a monitor (max 5 profiles, max 5 checks each).

panel_mode.description: True when sent from panel toggles
panel_mode.title: Panel Mode

return_schema.description: Check profile — defines which checks run per domain in a monitor.
return_schema.title: CheckProfileEntity

tools.name: list_check_profiles
tools.description: Show all check profiles — names and enabled check types. Call before create_monitor to pick the right profile_id.

params_schema.description: No parameters — satisfies V17 for parameterless handlers.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

CheckProfileEntity.description: Check profile — defines which checks run per domain in a monitor.
CheckProfileEntity.title: CheckProfileEntity

return_schema.description: Paginated check profile list — returned by list_check_profiles().
return_schema.title: CheckProfilePage

tools.name: update_check_profile
tools.description: Rename a check profile or replace its check type list. Use list_check_profiles first to get the profile_id.

params_schema.description: Update check profile — rename or change check types.
params_schema.title: UpdateProfileParams

return_schema.description: Check profile — defines which checks run per domain in a monitor.
return_schema.title: CheckProfileEntity

tools.name: delete_check_profile
tools.description: Permanently delete a check profile and all monitors that use it. Cannot be undone.

return_schema.description: Generic CRUD confirmation — create/update/delete operations.
return_schema.title: WtOpResult

tools.name: create_monitor
tools.description: Create a domain health monitor — saves a wt_monitors record that runs DNS/SSL/HTTP/email checks on a domain group at a recurring interval. Requires an existing domain group (group_id) and check profile (profile_id). NOT an automation rule. Max 5 monitors.

profile_id.description: Check profile ID defining which checks to run
profile_id.title: Profile Id

interval_hours.description: How often to run checks, in hours (1/6/12/24/48/168)
interval_hours.title: Interval Hours

return_schema.description: Domain health monitor — recurring scan configuration.
return_schema.title: MonitorEntity

tools.name: list_monitors
tools.description: Show all configured recurring domain monitors (scheduled automation setup). Use ONLY when user explicitly says: 'show my monitors', 'list monitors', 'what am I monitoring', 'manage monitors'. NEVER use this for: DNS lookups, domain checks, 'show records', 'check domain', 'what can webtools do on domain X', loading speed, SSL, blacklists — use dns_lookup, domain_full_check, or geo_check for all of those instead. Monitor NAMES in the database are irrelevant — a monitor named 'DNS monitor' does NOT mean this function should handle DNS record lookups.

params_schema.description: No parameters — satisfies V17 for parameterless handlers.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

MonitorEntity.description: Domain health monitor — recurring scan configuration.
MonitorEntity.title: MonitorEntity

return_schema.description: Paginated monitor list — returned by list_monitors().
return_schema.title: MonitorPage

tools.name: update_monitor
tools.description: Rename a monitor or change its scan interval. Does not change domains or checks — use update_domain_group or update_check_profile for that.

params_schema.description: Update monitor — rename or change interval.

interval_hours.description: New interval in hours (0 = keep current)
interval_hours.title: Interval Hours

params_schema.title: UpdateMonitorParams

return_schema.description: Domain health monitor — recurring scan configuration.
return_schema.title: MonitorEntity

tools.name: delete_monitor
tools.description: Delete a monitor. The domain group and check profile are preserved and can be reused.

return_schema.description: Generic CRUD confirmation — create/update/delete operations.
return_schema.title: WtOpResult

tools.name: create_monitor_full
tools.description: Create a complete health monitor in one step — name, domains, check types and interval. Atomically creates group + profile + monitor. Prefer this over create_monitor.

return_schema.description: Domain health monitor — recurring scan configuration.
return_schema.title: MonitorEntity

tools.name: quick_check
tools.description: Single-domain ad-hoc check from the panel. Presets: full=5 checks (dns+ssl+http+email+blacklist) in parallel, dns=DNS records, ssl=certificate quality grade, http=security headers grade, email=SPF/DMARC/DKIM, blacklist=spam lists, geo=reachability from EU/US/SG/MD (slowest), ports=TCP port scan. Result replaces left panel display.

domain.description: Domain name or IP address to check
domain.title: Domain

preset.description: Check type: full=5 checks parallel, or single check type
preset.title: Preset

return_schema.description: Bulk / quick scan operation result.
return_schema.title: ScanOpResult

tools.name: get_panel_data
tools.description: Panel data helper — returns counts and statuses for monitors, groups and profiles. Called by the panel on load; not needed in regular LLM chat.

params_schema.description: No parameters — satisfies V17 for parameterless handlers.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

return_schema.description: Panel summary — monitor/group/profile counts and health totals.
return_schema.title: PanelDataResult

tools.name: run_scan_tool
tools.description: Bulk domain scan (max 10) with chosen checks via toggles — results appear in the left panel. Use when user provides a list of domains to check simultaneously.

ssl.description: SSL certificate grade A-F and expiry days
ssl.title: Ssl

http.description: HTTP security headers grade A+ to F
http.title: Http

email.description: Email authentication: SPF, DMARC, DKIM grade
email.title: Email

smtp.description: SMTP server connectivity and STARTTLS support
smtp.title: Smtp

propagation.description: DNS propagation consistency across global resolvers
propagation.title: Propagation

return_schema.description: Bulk / quick scan operation result.
return_schema.title: ScanOpResult

tools.name: run_ip_scan
tools.description: Bulk IP scan (max 5) — geolocation + ASN, 29 DNSBL blacklist, reverse DNS (PTR), open ports, ping from EU/US/SG/MD. Use for IP-specific investigations.

ip_lookup.description: Geolocation: city, country, ASN for each IP
ip_lookup.title: Ip Lookup

blacklist.description: Spam blacklist: 30 DNSBL verdict for each IP
blacklist.title: Blacklist

reverse.description: Reverse DNS (PTR record) for each IP
reverse.title: Reverse

geo_ping.description: Ping latency from EU/US/SG/MD for each IP
geo_ping.title: Geo Ping

return_schema.description: Bulk / quick scan operation result.
return_schema.title: ScanOpResult

tools.name: audit_domains
tools.description: INSTANT bulk audit of MULTIPLE domains (up to 25) in ONE call — no monitors or setup required. Use when the user gives a LIST of domains to check/compare at once ('check these domains', 'audit all of these', 'compare these sites'). Runs the selected checks on every domain in parallel server-side and returns one result per domain. Default checks: dns + ssl + http + email + blacklist; add 'geo'/'whois'/'seo'/'ports'/'smtp'. For a SINGLE domain use domain_full_check instead. Do NOT use run_scan/list_monitors/get_scan_results — those are recurring-monitor tools.

domains.description: Domains to audit in one call (1..25). Duplicates are removed.
domains.title: Domains

checks.description: Checks to run per domain. Defaults to dns+ssl+http+email+blacklist. Add 'geo' for multi-region reachability (slowest), 'whois'/'seo'/'ports'/'smtp' as needed.
checks.title: Checks

include_raw.description: When false (default) each domain carries a compact per-check status + one-line summary. Set true to also include full raw check data (large).
include_raw.title: Include Raw

DomainAuditResult.description: Full parallel domain audit — all check results combined.
DomainAuditResult.title: DomainAuditResult

return_schema.description: Bulk multi-domain audit — one DomainAuditResult per domain.
return_schema.title: DomainAuditPage
